title: Security+ SY0-701 — Section 17: Network Security
description: Jason Dion Security+ SY0-701 — Section 17: Network Security. Domain: Security Architecture (3.0).

questions.question: What is defense in depth?
answers: A layered security approach using multiple controls to protect assets
Using one very strong firewall
Encrypting all network traffic
Hiring more security personnel
questions.explanation: Defense in depth uses multiple security layers (physical, technical, administrative) so that if one control fails, others continue to protect the asset.

questions.question: What is a stateful firewall?
answers: A firewall that tracks the state of active connections to make filtering decisions
A firewall that only filters based on IP addresses
A firewall that inspects application-layer data
A firewall that logs all traffic
questions.explanation: A stateful firewall monitors the state of active network connections and makes filtering decisions based on context (e.g., allowing return traffic for established sessions).

questions.question: What capabilities distinguish a Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) from a traditional firewall?
answers: Application awareness, deep packet inspection, and integrated intrusion prevention
Higher bandwidth throughput only
Wireless network support
Cloud-based management only
questions.explanation: NGFWs go beyond port/protocol filtering to include application identification, DPI, SSL inspection, and integrated IPS capabilities.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a DMZ (Demilitarized Zone) in network security?
answers: To isolate public-facing servers from the internal network
To connect remote workers to the office
To store backup data
To separate wireless from wired networks
questions.explanation: A DMZ is a network segment that sits between the internet and the internal network, hosting public-facing services (web servers, mail servers) while limiting access to internal systems.

questions.question: How do VLANs improve network security?
answers: By logically segmenting networks to limit broadcast domains and contain breaches
By encrypting all VLAN traffic
By increasing network speed
By replacing the need for firewalls
questions.explanation: VLANs create logical network segments on physical infrastructure, isolating traffic between departments or security zones to contain lateral movement.

questions.question: What is the key difference between IDS and IPS?
answers: IDS detects and alerts; IPS detects and actively blocks threats
IDS is hardware; IPS is software
IDS is newer than IPS
IDS works on endpoints; IPS works on networks
questions.explanation: An Intrusion Detection System (IDS) monitors and alerts on suspicious activity. An Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) can also actively block or drop malicious traffic.

questions.question: What is the core principle of Zero Trust networking?
answers: Never trust, always verify — no implicit trust based on network location
Trust internal network users but verify external users
Trust all users after initial authentication
Zero Trust means blocking all traffic by default
questions.explanation: Zero Trust assumes breach and requires continuous verification of every user, device, and connection regardless of network location, replacing the 'trusted internal network' model.

questions.question: What does Network Access Control (NAC) do?
answers: Enforces security policies before allowing devices to connect to the network
Controls internet bandwidth usage
Manages DNS records
Monitors network performance
questions.explanation: NAC solutions check devices for compliance (patching, antivirus, configuration) before granting network access, quarantining non-compliant devices.

questions.question: Why is network segmentation a security best practice?
answers: It limits lateral movement by isolating segments, containing breaches
It increases network speed significantly
It reduces the need for firewalls
It simplifies network management
questions.explanation: Network segmentation divides the network into zones, so a compromised segment cannot easily be used to attack other segments, limiting the blast radius of an attack.

questions.question: What is a VPN and how does it protect data?
answers: A Virtual Private Network creates an encrypted tunnel for secure remote communication
A Virtual Private Network speeds up internet connections
A tool for anonymizing web browsing only
A network switch configuration
questions.explanation: A VPN encrypts data in transit between endpoints, creating a secure tunnel over untrusted networks like the internet.

questions.question: How does a forward proxy improve security?
answers: It intermediates client requests, filtering content and hiding internal IP addresses
It only increases network speed
It provides additional network storage
It replaces the need for a firewall
questions.explanation: A forward proxy sits between internal clients and the internet, enforcing content filtering, caching responses, and masking internal network details from external servers.

questions.question: What is DNS sinkholeing used for?
answers: Redirecting malicious domain requests to a controlled IP to block threats
Caching DNS records for faster resolution
Encrypting DNS traffic
Monitoring DNS query performance
questions.explanation: DNS sinkholeing redirects traffic destined for known malicious domains to a controlled sinkhole IP, preventing malware command-and-control communications.

questions.question: What does port security on a network switch do?
answers: Restricts which MAC addresses can connect to a physical switch port
Encrypts traffic on the port
Limits bandwidth usage per port
Monitors for intrusions on each port
questions.explanation: Switch port security limits the MAC addresses that can connect to a switch port, preventing unauthorized devices from plugging into the network.

questions.question: What does SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) do?
answers: Aggregates and analyzes security logs from across the network for threat detection
Manages user identities and access
Scans endpoints for malware
Encrypts network communications
questions.explanation: SIEM collects, correlates, and analyzes log data from multiple sources to detect security incidents, generate alerts, and support compliance reporting.

questions.question: What security benefit does SD-WAN offer over traditional WAN?
answers: Centralized policy enforcement and encrypted traffic across all WAN links
Higher bandwidth at lower cost only
Eliminates the need for network security tools
Provides physical security for network equipment
questions.explanation: SD-WAN enables centralized security policy management, built-in encryption, and consistent controls across distributed branch locations.
